text: Explain what a Rust borrow checker does in 2 sentences

blocks.text: Explain what a Rust borrow checker does in 2 sentences

The Rust borrow checker is a compile-time system that ensures memory safety by enforcing ownership rules—each value has one owner, and the checker prevents multiple mutable references or mixing mutable and immutable references to the same data simultaneously. It eliminates entire classes of bugs like use-after-free, data races, and buffer overflows without needing a garbage collector, making Rust both safe and performant.
